paper_title: Consequences of dam construction upstream of the Upper Paraná River floodplain (Brazil): a temporal analysis of the Chironomidae community over an eight-year period.

methods.description: This method describes the collection and processing of zoobenthos samples from the Upper Paraná River floodplain over an eight-year period to analyze the Chironomidae community.

steps.description: Zoobenthos were collected from 2000 to 2007, with three samplings conducted in 2001, two in 2003 and four between 2004 and 2007.
steps.searchable_summary: collect zoobenthos samples over specified years

steps.description: Four samples were taken using a Petersen grab at each sampling site: three for biological analyses and one for sedimentological analyses.
steps.searchable_summary: use Petersen grab to collect samples for analysis

steps.description: Values of water temperature (°C), dissolved oxygen concentration (mg.L -1 ), pH and conductivity (µS.cm -1 ) were recorded at each sampling site.
steps.searchable_summary: record water quality parameters at sampling sites

steps.description: Data on the hydrometric levels were provided by the ANA (Agência Nacional de Águas).
steps.searchable_summary: obtain hydrometric level data from ANA

steps.description: Sediment samples were dried in an oven at 80 °C.
The granulometric texture was determined according to the Wentworth's scale (1922), using the wet method.
steps.searchable_summary: determine granulometric texture using Wentworth's scale

steps.description: Organic matter content in the sediment was obtained by calcination of the samples in a muffle at 560 °C for approximately four hours.
steps.searchable_summary: calculate organic matter content by calcination

steps.description: Biological material was washed through a set of sieves (2.0, 1.0, and 0.2 mm mesh sizes).
The organisms found in the 2.0 and 1.0 mm mesh sieves were immediately fixed in 70% alcohol.
The material retained in the 0.2 mm mesh sieve was placed in polyethylene jars containing 70% alcohol.
The material was observed under a stereoscopic microscope at Nupélia/UEM Zoobenthos Laboratory.
Chironomidae larvae were identified down to the lowest taxonomic level possible using the identification keys of Epler (1992) and Trivinho-Strixino and Strixino (1995).
steps.searchable_summary: identify Chironomidae larvae using identification keys
